---
title: "Project 1 Final Report"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Project 1: Linear Regression |
| **Due** | Week 12 |
| **Weight** | 40% of Project 1 (which is 20% of final grade) |
| **Related Guide** | [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
Use the [Project 1 guidelines]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch02-guidelines-linear.html){target="_blank"} for the 7-part structure and page budgets, the [Project 1 checklist]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch02-guidelines-linear.html#sec-linear-checklist){target="_blank"} before submitting, and the [worked Project 1 results chapter]({{< var books.project_guide >}}/chapters/ch05-linear-results.html){target="_blank"} as a model for polished tables, diagnostics, interpretation, and discussion.
:::

## Purpose

The final report is a polished, complete linear regression analysis that demonstrates your ability to formulate a research question, select and fit appropriate models, diagnose problems, and communicate statistical findings clearly. This submission should incorporate all feedback received on your proposal and draft.

## Requirements

Your final report should follow the 7-part structure in the Project Guide. Use the checklist below as a short website summary; the guide is the detailed source for what each part should contain.

### 1. Background and Research Questions

- Motivate the topic
- State 2--3 numbered research questions
- Keep dataset details for Part 2

### 2. Methods: Subjects and Variables

- Describe the data source, study design, sample, exclusions, missingness, and final analytic sample size
- Define the outcome, predictors, coding decisions, reference categories, and transformations

### 3. Methods: Statistical Analysis

- State the planned linear regression model or model sequence
- Link each model to specific research questions
- State that nested models use the same complete-case analysis dataset

### 4. Results: Descriptive Analyses

- Present descriptive tables and targeted figures using the complete-case analysis dataset
- Describe distributions, group differences, missingness, and potential issues

### 5. Results: Regression Analyses

- Present a publication-style regression table with coefficients, standard errors, confidence intervals, *p*-values, and fit statistics
- Summarize model comparisons, diagnostics, and substantive interpretations

### 6. Discussion

- Answer each research question in plain language
- Discuss practical significance, limitations, and appropriate next steps

### 7. Appendix and Reproducibility Materials

- Include supporting diagnostic output that would interrupt the main text
- Submit an annotated `.qmd` or `.R` source file that recreates the analysis from a clean R session

## Formatting

- Use clear headings and subheadings
- Number all tables and figures with descriptive captions
- Report statistics using proper notation
- Main text for Parts 1--6 should be approximately 6 single-spaced pages; tables, figures, and appendix materials are in addition to this limit
- Follow the Project 1 section-by-section page budgets in the Project Guide

## Submission

Submit the following to Blackboard:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your annotated `.qmd` or `.R` source file
- The dataset, a link to the dataset, or clear instructions for accessing it
